description: Loket-native WhatsApp channel adapter (Meta Cloud API) — a dumb pipe that forwards each message to a target agent over the loket bus and relays the reply. WhatsApp Cloud API has NO polling: inbound arrives via webhook. This connector is webhook-driven — it self-provisions its webhook secret on boot so the host intake (/api/kernel/webhook/whatsapp-channel) accepts Meta's POST, then parses + forwards + replies via the Cloud API. Idle (no send) until a token is set.
author: @flowork-os

exposes_rpc.name: boot
exposes_rpc.description: Self-provision the webhook secret into own store (so the host webhook intake accepts Meta's POST), then exit clean. WhatsApp is webhook-driven, so there is no polling loop. Idle when no secret is configured.

exposes_rpc.name: handle_webhook
exposes_rpc.description: Parse ONE Meta WhatsApp webhook payload: for each text message, forward {text,from} to the target agent over the bus and reply via the Cloud API. This is what the host intake invokes for each delivery.

exposes_rpc.name: handle_update
exposes_rpc.description: Process ONE synthetic update (testable core): forward {text,from,target} to the agent over the bus and return the reply. send:true relays to WhatsApp when a token is set.
